answer:
\boxed{D}

This condition describes **Alexia without agraphia**, where disconnection of the occipital lobe (visual centers) from the language areas (typically via the splenium of the corpus callosum) impairs reading ability while sparing writing. The patient retains the ability to write but cannot read their own writing or external text. This syndrome is distinct from aphasia (A, B, I) or agnosia (J), which involve broader language or perceptual deficits.
